description: Full dependency upgrade to latest versions: Next.js 16, React 19, Prisma 7, next-auth v5, tRPC stable v11, Tailwind CSS 4, ESLint 9 flat config

userStories.title: Upgrade React 18 to 19 and Next.js 14 to 16
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upgrade React and Next.js to their latest major versions so the project uses Turbopack and React 19 features.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: react and react-dom updated to ^19.2.4
In package.json: next updated to ^16.1.0
In package.json: @types/react updated to ^19.0.0 and @types/react-dom updated to ^19.0.0
In package.json: eslint-config-next updated to ^16.1.0
In package.json: @t3-oss/env-nextjs updated to latest compatible version
In src/trpc/server.ts: headers() call updated to await headers() (async in Next.js 15+)
next.config.js renamed to next.config.ts with TypeScript NextConfig type
pnpm install completes without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Next.js 16 requires Node.js >= 20.9.0. The headers() function from next/headers became async in Next.js 15. The createContext function in src/trpc/server.ts wraps the headers() call and must handle the async change.

userStories.title: Upgrade Prisma 6 to 7 - Schema and Config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to update the Prisma schema and create the new prisma.config.ts so the project is configured for Prisma 7.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: prisma (dev) and @prisma/client updated to ^7.5.0
In package.json: @prisma/adapter-ppg added as dependency
In package.json: tsx added as dev dependency
In prisma/schema.prisma: generator provider changed from prisma-client-js to prisma-client
In prisma/schema.prisma: output = "./generated" added to generator client block
In prisma/schema.prisma: url = env("DATABASE_URL") removed from datasource db block
In prisma/schema.prisma: provider = "postgresql" preserved in datasource db block
Any previewFeatures = ["driverAdapters"] removed from schema if present
prisma.config.ts created at repo root using defineConfig and env from prisma/config with datasource.url set to env("DATABASE_URL")
pnpm install completes without errors
prisma generate succeeds and outputs to ./prisma/generated/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the official Prisma v7 migration guide. The prisma.config.ts must import dotenv/config at the top. The generator output path ./generated means the client is generated to prisma/generated/. Do NOT change src/server/db.ts yet - that is the next story.

userStories.title: Upgrade Prisma 7 - Rewrite DB Client with Driver Adapter
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to rewrite the Prisma client initialization to use the v7 driver adapter pattern so database connections use direct TCP.
acceptanceCriteria: src/server/db.ts rewritten to use PrismaPPG adapter from @prisma/adapter-ppg
PrismaClient import path in src/server/db.ts changed from @prisma/client to the generated client path (prisma/generated/prisma/client.js or equivalent via path alias)
PrismaPPG adapter instantiated with connectionString from env.DATABASE_URL
Dev singleton pattern (globalForPrisma) preserved in src/server/db.ts
Logging config preserved: query+error+warn in dev, error only in production
All other files importing types from @prisma/client updated to use the generated client path
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The generated client path depends on the output setting in schema.prisma. With output = "./generated", the import should be something like ../../prisma/generated/prisma/client.js relative to src/server/db.ts, or use the ~ path alias if it resolves correctly. Check src/server/auth.ts and src/server/api/trpc.ts for any @prisma/client imports that need updating.

userStories.title: Upgrade next-auth v4 to v5 - Rewrite Auth Config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to rewrite the auth configuration to use the Auth.js v5 NextAuth() pattern so the project uses the modern universal auth() API.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: next-auth updated to latest v5 beta (next-auth@beta)
In package.json: @auth/prisma-adapter updated to latest compatible version
src/server/auth.ts rewritten to use NextAuth() function that exports { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut }
Discord provider preserved with env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID and env.DISCORD_CLIENT_SECRET
PrismaAdapter(db) preserved using @auth/prisma-adapter
Session callback preserved: adds user.id to session object
Module augmentation updated for next-auth v5 types
src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts simplified to: import { handlers } from ~/server/auth and export const { GET, POST } = handlers
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth.js v5 uses a different API pattern. Instead of exporting authOptions and calling getServerSession(authOptions), you call NextAuth({ providers, adapter, callbacks }) which returns { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut }. The auth() function is the universal session getter that replaces getServerSession.

userStories.title: Update Auth Consumers - tRPC Context and Env Vars
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to update all code that consumes the auth API so it uses the new v5 auth() function and updated environment variable names.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/server/api/trpc.ts: import changed from getServerAuthSession to auth from ~/server/auth
In src/server/api/trpc.ts: session retrieval changed from await getServerAuthSession() to await auth()
In src/server/api/trpc.ts: unused import * as trpcNext from @trpc/server/adapters/next removed
In src/env.js: NEXTAUTH_SECRET renamed to AUTH_SECRET in server schema and runtimeEnv
In src/env.js: NEXTAUTH_URL renamed to AUTH_URL in server schema and runtimeEnv
If .env or .env.example exists: variable names updated from NEXTAUTH_SECRET to AUTH_SECRET and NEXTAUTH_URL to AUTH_URL
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Auth.js v5 uses AUTH_SECRET instead of NEXTAUTH_SECRET and AUTH_URL instead of NEXTAUTH_URL. The env validation in src/env.js must be updated to match. The tRPC context in src/server/api/trpc.ts calls getServerAuthSession() which must be changed to auth().

userStories.title: Upgrade tRPC from RC to Stable v11
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upgrade tRPC from release candidate 11.0.0-rc.446 to stable v11 so the project uses the production-ready API layer.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: @trpc/client updated to ^11.6.0
In package.json: @trpc/server updated to ^11.6.0
In package.json: @trpc/react-query updated to ^11.6.0
In package.json: @tanstack/react-query updated to ^5.90.21
If unstable_httpBatchStreamLink was renamed to httpBatchStreamLink in stable v11, update the import in src/trpc/react.tsx
Verify createHydrationHelpers import from @trpc/react-query/rsc works in src/trpc/server.ts (fix if needed)
Verify superjson transformer config in src/server/api/trpc.ts still works (fix if needed)
pnpm install completes without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The classic @trpc/react-query integration is still supported in tRPC v11 stable, so no need to migrate to @trpc/tanstack-react-query. The main risk is unstable_ prefixed APIs being renamed. Check the tRPC v11 changelog for any breaking changes from the RC.

userStories.title: Upgrade Tailwind CSS 3 to 4
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upgrade Tailwind CSS from v3 to v4 so the project uses the new CSS-first configuration approach.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: tailwindcss updated to ^4.0.0
In package.json: @tailwindcss/postcss added if needed for Next.js PostCSS integration
postcss removed from package.json if only used for Tailwind, OR postcss config updated to use @tailwindcss/postcss
tailwindcss-animate removed or replaced with Tailwind v4 compatible alternative
prettier-plugin-tailwindcss updated to latest in package.json
Tailwind config file (tailwind.config.ts or similar) deleted or contents migrated to CSS-first @theme config
postcss.config.js or postcss.config.cjs deleted or updated to use @tailwindcss/postcss plugin
src/app/globals.css updated: replace @tailwind directives with @import "tailwindcss" and move any custom theme to @theme block
All existing utility classes verified to compile (fix any v4 renames if needed)
pnpm install completes without errors
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Tailwind CSS 4 uses CSS-first configuration instead of JS config files. The @tailwind base/components/utilities directives are replaced with @import "tailwindcss". Custom theme values move into @theme {} blocks in CSS. Some utility classes may have been renamed. Check tailwind.config.ts for any custom theme configuration that needs to be migrated.

userStories.title: Upgrade ESLint 8 to 9 with Flat Config
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upgrade ESLint from v8 to v9 with the new flat config format so the project uses modern linting configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: eslint updated to ^9.0.0
In package.json: @typescript-eslint/eslint-plugin and @typescript-eslint/parser updated to latest compatible versions
.eslintrc.cjs deleted
eslint.config.js or eslint.config.mjs created with ESLint 9 flat config format
next/core-web-vitals config included (from eslint-config-next)
typescript-eslint recommended-type-checked and stylistic-type-checked configs included

All existing custom rules preserved: consistent-type-imports with inline-type-imports, no-unused-vars with argsIgnorePattern ^_, require-await off, no-misused-promises with checksVoidReturn attributes false
pnpm lint runs without config errors (pre-existing code warnings are acceptable)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: ESLint 9 uses flat config (eslint.config.js) instead of .eslintrc files. The eslint-config-next package for Next.js 16 should support flat config. If not, use @eslint/eslintrc compatibility wrapper. The typescript-eslint package provides a unified way to include TS rules in flat config.

userStories.title: Upgrade Remaining Dependencies
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to upgrade all remaining dependencies to their latest compatible versions for security and feature improvements.
acceptanceCriteria: In package.json: sharp updated to ^0.34.5
In package.json: zod updated to latest ^3.x
In package.json: superjson updated to latest ^2.x
In package.json: geist updated to latest
In package.json: lucide-react updated to latest
In package.json: class-variance-authority updated to latest
In package.json: clsx updated to latest
In package.json: tailwind-merge updated to latest
In package.json: image-to-ico updated to latest
In package.json: server-only updated to latest
In package.json: all @radix-ui/* packages updated to latest React 19-compatible versions
In package.json: prettier updated to latest
In package.json: @types/node updated to ^22.x
In package.json: typescript updated to latest ^5.x
Sharp API in src/server/api/routers/convert.ts verified (webp/png/jpeg/ico conversion still works)
Radix UI wrapper components in src/components/ui/ updated if forwardRef patterns changed for React 19
pnpm install completes without errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are mostly minor/patch updates. The sharp 0.33→0.34 update is the one to watch for API changes — verify the sharp(buffer).webp()/.png()/.jpeg() patterns in the convert router. Radix UI components may need forwardRef pattern updates for React 19 compatibility.
